Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.1 Rites of Passage: childhood rites
Content_Standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of his/her culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures.
Indicator: B7.1.1.1.1 Identify the processes involved in naming a child.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Discuss the outdooring and the naming process of a child in their community. E.g. outdooring/ushering the baby, invitation of ancestors, giving the baby a name, blessing, presentation of gifts. 2. Identify the items used in the naming process and write them. 3. Explain the significance of the items used for the naming ceremony.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Demonstrate behaviour and skills of working towards group goals) (Identify and analyse different points of views of speaker)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.1 Rites of Passage: childhood rites
Content_Standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of his/her culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures.
Indicator: B7.1.1.1.2 Discuss the significance of the processes in naming a child
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Read a one hundred and fifty words passage on naming ceremony in his or her culture. 2. Discuss the significance of the processes involved in naming a child as raised in the passage. 3. Discuss libation text used during naming ceremony. 4. Write the three main parts of a libation text.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Demonstrate behaviour and skills of working towards group goals) (Identify and analyse different points of views of speaker)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.1 Rites of Passage: childhood rites
Content_Standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of his/her culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures.
Indicator: B7.1.1.1.3 Compare and contrast the naming processes in his/her culture to other Ghanaian cultures.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Discuss the naming process of one other culture of Ghana and compare with the naming process of his/her people. 2. Write about commonalities and differences among the cultures.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Explain ideas in a clear order with relevant detail, using conjunctions to structure and speech)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.1 Rites of Passage: childhood rites
Content_Standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of his/her culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures.
Indicator: B7.1.1.1.4 Compare and contrast the traditional naming process to the contemporary naming process.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Discuss the contemporary naming processes and compare it with the traditional naming processes in Ghana. 2. Show the similarities and differences.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Explain ideas in a clear order with relevant detail, using conjunctions to structure and speech)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.1 Rites of Passage: childhood rites
Content_Standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the childhood rites of his/her culture and compare with other Ghanaian cultures.
Indicator: B7.1.1.1.5 Role play a naming ceremony session in his/her culture.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Role play a naming ceremony among your people. 2. Discuss the role play and what they have learnt from it.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Effectively perform multiple roles within the group) (Speak clearly and explain ideas. Share a narrative or extended answer while speaking to a group)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.2 Naming Systems: Day names, order of birth names
Content_Standard: B7.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of day names and order of birth names and relate the names to the days and order.
Indicator: B7.1.2.1.1 State the names of the days of the week and relate their names to the days.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. State the names of the days of the week and important occasion (e.g festival) months of the year in the Ghanaian language of study. 2. Relate the names of the days of the week or months of the year to human names.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Provide feedback in areas of ideas, organisation, voice, word choice and sentence fluency in communication)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.1.2 Naming Systems: Day names, order of birth names
Content_Standard: B7.1.2.1 Exhibit understanding of day names and order of birth names and relate the names to the days and order.
Indicator: B7.1.2.1.2 State the order of birth (i.e. first, second, and so on.) and relate the names accordingly.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. State the order of birth of his or her culture. E.g. first born, second born, and so on. 2. Relate the names to the order of birth.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Identify underlying themes, implications and issues when listening)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B7.1.3.1: Demonstrate an understanding of the clan system among their people.
Indicator: B7.1.3.1.1 Describe the clan system and state some clans in his/her community.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Explain what a clan is. 2. State some clans you know.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Explain ideas in a clear order with relevant detail, using conjunctions to structure and speech.)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of how chiefs and queenmothers are selected, enstooled/enskinned and compare their duties and responsibilities.
Indicator: B7.1.4.1.1 Discuss how chiefs and queenmothers are selected in his/her ethnic community.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Read a passage of about three to four paragraphs on how chiefs and queenmothers are selected in your community. 2. Identify the traditional leaders mentioned in the passage. 3. Relate how chiefs and queenmothers are selected among your people to the passage read.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Demonstrate behaviour and skills of working towards group goals)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B7.2.1.1: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations
Indicator: B7.2.1.1.1. Use appropriate register in everyday communication (informal and formal) with diverse partners on grade-level topics/texts/issues
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Identify formal situations. 2. Use appropriate language to participate in formal interactions. 3. Identify informal situations. 4. Use appropriate language to participate in conversations in the following situations: Greetings, requests, encouragements, partings.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Provide feedback in areas of ideas, organisation, voice, word choice and sentence fluency in communication)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B7.2.2.1: Demonstrate the ability to listen to extended reading and identify key information
Indicator: B7.1.2.1.1. Listen to a level-appropriate text attentively and identify key information.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Identify key points: intent or purpose of the message, assessing the speaker's enthusiasm and passion for the topic. 2. Read out and cross check the information identify main ideas and supporting points in the text.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Provide feedback in areas of ideas, organisation, voice, word choice and sentence fluency in communication)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B7.3.1.1 Understand the main ideas and supporting points in texts'
Indicator: B7.3.1.1.1 Read and understand main ideas and supporting points in a range of texts on familiar and unfamiliar topics.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Skim a given passage of about one hundred and fifty words and state the main ideas. 2. Identify the supporting ideas in the text. 3. Tell the relationship between the main ideas and supporting ideas. 4. Answer recall and inferential questions concerning the text read.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Apply appropriate diction and structure sentences correctly for narrative, persuasive, imaginative and expository purposes)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B7.4.1 Sentences: Simple, Compound and Complex
Content_Standard: B7.4.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the components of sentences.
Indicator: B7.4.1.1.1 Discuss the components of sentences.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Explain what a sentence is. 2. Give examples of sentences and their component parts in his/her language. 3. Discuss the components of a sentence in your language.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Explain ideas in a clear order with relevant detail, using conjunctions to structure and speech.)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B8.1.1 Rites of Passage: puberty rites
Content_Standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how puberty rites are performed in his/her linguistic community, its significance and compare with other cultures of Ghana.
Indicator: B8.1.1.1.1 Identify the processes involved in performing puberty rites in your culture.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Explain what puberty rites are. 2. Talk about the items needed for the performance of puberty rites among his/her people and their significance. 3. Discuss the processes involved in performing puberty rites for boys/girls among his/her people. 4. Read and discuss a two-hundred-word passage on puberty rites. 5. Explain the significance of puberty rites. 6. Role play a puberty rite activity.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Identify underlying themes, implications and issues when listening) Cultural Identity (Show a strong sense of belongingness to one's culture)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B8.1.2 Naming Systems: family names, kinship terms, proverbial and insinuation names
Content_Standard: B8.1.2.1 Recognise and understand family names, kinship terms and proverbial and insinuation names.
Indicator: B8.1.2.1.1 Discuss the sources of family names in your language and use them appropriately.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Read a text on names and their sources. 2. Mention family names and their sources. E.g. clan, grand and great-grand parents, etc. 3. Discuss the importance of family names. 4. Make a documentation on family names for male and female and indicate their sources.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Demonstrate behaviour and skills of working towards group goals) Cultural Identity (Develop and exhibit the sense of cultural identity)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B8.2.5.1 Demonstrate knowledge of presentational skills in listening, following daily conversation, speaking clearly and explaining ideas.
Indicator: B8.2.5.1.1 Develop and modify his/her language in narrating some selected daily activities.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Narrate what you do at home every Saturday. 2. Describe what you saw on your last visit to a market. 3. Draw and discuss a scenery on a given topic (e.g.: fishing, farming, weaving, etc.). 4. Describe a visit to the hospital. 5. Describe a scene at a lorry station.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and collaboration (Speak clearly and explain ideas. Share a narrative or extended answer while speaking to a group)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Content_Standard: B9.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of marriage of his/her culture and compare with other cultures of Ghana.
Indicator: B9.1.1.1.1 Discuss the processes involved in performing marriage rites in his/her culture.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Explain what marriage is. 2. Read and discuss a text on marriage. 3. Identify types of marriage. 4. Identify some of the marriage rites in his/her culture and write them. 5. Discuss marriage rites performance. 6. Explore the significance of the processes involved in performing marriage rites.
Core_Competencies_Values: Communication and Collaboration (Provide feedback in areas of ideas, organisation, voice, word choice and sentence fluency in communication) Cultural Identity (Develop and exhibit the sense of cultural identity)

Subject: Ghanaian Language
Sub_Strand_Number_and_Name: B10.1.1 Rites of Passage: death and funeral rites
Content_Standard: B10.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of issues relating to death and funeral rites of his/her culture and compare with other cultures of Ghana.
Indicator: B10.1.1.1.1 Discuss the types and causes of death in your culture.
Phase_2_Main_Exemplers: 1. Read and discuss a text on death. 2. Discuss and write the types of death. 3. Explore the causes of the different types of death. 4. Examine the effects of unnatural death in his/her culture. 5. Compose a piece on ways of curbing unnatural death.
Core_Competencies_Values: Cultural identity and Global citizenship (Show a strong sense of belongingness to one's culture)
